scope_note: QA Pisano mod-8 CRT cert [302]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.X (Fibonacci) and Ch.V (CRT); Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci Primitive Roots, Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano periods). All computations use exact Fibonacci matrix M^k=[[F(k-1),F(k)],[F(k),F(k+1)]]. CLAIM: (C1) pi(8)=12: Fibonacci mod 8 has period exactly 12; F(12)=144 equiv 0, F(13)=233 equiv 1 mod 8; M^12 equiv I mod 8; M^k not I mod 8 for k in {1,2,3,4,6}. (C2) M^6 equiv 5I mod 8: F(5)=5, F(6)=0 mod 8; M^6=[[5,0],[0,5]] mod 8; 5^2=25 equiv 1 mod 8. (C3) CRT: pi(24)=lcm(pi(8),pi(3))=lcm(12,8)=24; verified M^24 equiv I mod 24, order is exactly 24. (C4) M^12 mod 24 = 17I: F(11)=89 equiv 17 mod 24, F(12)=144 equiv 0 mod 24; 17^2=289 equiv 1 mod 24. Three grade scalars: mod 9 gives 8 equiv -1 (cert [298]), mod 8 gives 5 at step 6 (C2), mod 24 gives 17 at step 12 (C4); CRT: 17 equiv -1 mod 9 and 17 equiv 1 mod 8. (C5) Exact order 24 in GL(2,Z/24Z): M^24 equiv I; M^k not I for k in {1,2,3,4,6,8,12}. Builds on cert [291] (pi(9)=24), cert [298] (M^12=-I mod 9), cert [301] (pi(3)=8). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
